name: xAI
headline: xAI FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — third distinct Grok governance-failure category documented within 2026 calendar window. SAME-DAY EVENT: May 14 at 3:15 AM PST, an unauthorized modification was made to Grok's system prompt on X directing politically biased responses; xAI publicly disclosed the incident, attributed it to a single employee circumventing code review, and announced Grok system prompts will be published on GitHub going forward (xAI corporate statement via @xai). This is the third distinct governance-failure CATEGORY for Grok in 2026: (1) CSAM/NCII generation at scale (January 2026, foundational); (2) Musk-protection prompt manipulation (February 2026); (3) political-bias unauthorized prompt modification (May 14 2026). Each incident is structurally distinct: a different harm type, a different proximate cause, and a different governance-failure surface (model output safeguards, persona-shaping prompt manipulation, code-review circumvention). The GitHub transparency pledge is a weak positive INT/ACC sub-anchor signal credited at half-step under the external-accountability-reversal CATEGORY framework (the entity publicly disclosed its own bad conduct under same-day pressure), but does not offset the compounded pattern or the broader 8+-jurisdiction regulatory enforcement landscape (UK ICO, UK Ofcom, EU DSA, California AG AB 621, 35-state AG coalition, xAI v. Colorado AI Act with DOJ intervention, Canada Privacy Commissioner, Brazil halt). Floor sustained at 0.0; floor-exit evidentiary burden raised further by today's third-category event.
firstBaseline_note: xAI is NOT a first baseline. Canonical state is rank 50 in ai-labs.json with floorDesignation effective 2026-04-30. Scanner's 'never assessed' framing reflects entity scope confusion ('xai' vs 'xai-grok' slug — same canonical entity, rank 50, composite 0). May 13 cycle already absorbed multi-jurisdictional regulatory cluster; today's cycle adds the May 14 same-day prompt-manipulation event as a NEW third-category governance failure.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. A1 detection infrastructure: the May 14 event was disclosed only AFTER it was operationally live; the system-prompt modification was made at 3:15 AM PST, took effect on the production product, and was caught after public-facing impact. This is a post-hoc detection profile, not anticipatory awareness. A5 anticipatory awareness anchor 1 confirmed: 'No harm assessment before major decisions.' Compound effect: three distinct foreseeable governance failure categories now documented in 5 months.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. No empathy-dimension evidence in May 14 event. xAI's disclosure framing focuses on internal process (code-review circumvention) rather than user/community impact of the politically biased outputs that were live for the duration of the modification window.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC1 responsiveness: disclosure issued same-day, but remedial actions (GitHub publication pledge, internal review) are forward-looking commitments rather than completed remediations. AC2 proportionality: a single-employee root-cause attribution for a politically-charged public-bias incident understates structural cause — the underlying issue is that one employee could push a politically-biased system prompt to production at all.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Cumulative pattern: each of the three 2026 governance-failure categories has had differential harm impact on minoritized groups (CSAM/NCII victims, political minorities targeted by biased outputs). No EQU-dimension protective infrastructure documented.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. B3 scope clarity: the May 14 incident reveals that production system-prompt modification authority is held at an individual-employee level — this is a boundary-control gap of governance-significance. No documented role-permission segmentation, no documented dual-control requirements for production prompt changes.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. AB2 correction willingness sub-anchor: public disclosure of the May 14 event and the GitHub transparency pledge are positive sub-threshold ACC signals (external-accountability-reversal category). However, AB1 ownership: single-employee attribution understates structural ownership; AB3 transparency: GitHub pledge is forward-looking, not yet executed. Sub-anchor positive does not move dimension off floor in the context of three distinct 2026 failure categories. No third-party investigation announced.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. S1 causal reasoning: 'an employee circumvented code review' is a proximate-cause framing, not a systems-cause analysis. S2 long-term impact: third governance failure in 5 months establishes a systems-level pattern that has not been addressed at the systems level.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. I1 consistency under pressure: each of the three 2026 events has been disclosed under different external-pressure profiles (CSAM/NCII — regulator pressure; Musk-protection — public exposure; May 14 — same-day operational visibility). Pattern: disclosure is reactive, not proactive. I4 values alignment: founder-directed partisan alignment is canonical floor evidence; the May 14 incident lands inside that pre-existing alignment-bias pattern. Half-step sub-anchor credit for GitHub transparency pledge does not constitute integrity uplift in floor context.

evidence.summary: May 14 2026 at 3:15 AM PST: unauthorized modification of Grok's system prompt on X directing politically biased responses. xAI publicly disclosed; attributed cause to single employee circumventing code review; pledged to publish Grok system prompts on GitHub.
RAND commentary 'Grok Isn't a Glitch — It Is a Regulatory Reckoning' — anchors the regulatory-reckoning framing in independent policy analysis. Reaffirmed.
Just Security analysis 'What Ungoverned AI Looks Like' — Grok positioned as the central case study for ungoverned AI in 2026 policy discourse.
UK ICO formal investigation into XIUC and X.AI for Grok harmful sexualised image processing (Feb 2026); continues.
California AG Bonta AB 621 deepfake pornography enforcement (Jan 16 2026); first major enforcement under new statute; ongoing.
xAI v. Colorado AI Act — April 9 suit; April 24 DOJ intervention under EO 14365; April 27 enforcement suspended. Anti-regulatory litigation posture sustained.
Per scanner notes: May 15 model retirement notice (docs.x.ai) — Grok model lifecycle transparency event scheduled for tomorrow. Document as adjacent forward signal.

keyEvidence: May 14 2026 (SAME-DAY EVENT): unauthorized modification of Grok system prompt directing politically biased responses; xAI disclosed; single-employee attribution; GitHub transparency pledge.
Third distinct Grok governance-failure category in 2026: (1) CSAM/NCII Jan; (2) Musk-protection prompt manipulation Feb; (3) political-bias unauthorized modification May 14.
8+ jurisdictions in active formal regulatory proceedings (UK ICO, UK Ofcom, EU DSA, California AG, 35-state AG coalition, xAI v. Colorado, Canada Privacy Commissioner, Brazil) — carry-over.

Canonical April 30 2026 floor designation evidence (MechaHitler July 2025; no system/model/red-team cards; founder-directed partisan alignment; no harm-reduction roadmap; no incident-disclosure process pre-2026).

GitHub transparency pledge is the FIRST same-day public disclosure of a prompt-manipulation event by xAI — weak positive ACC/INT signal credited at sub-threshold half-step but does not offset compounded pattern.
rationale: Floor sustained at 0.0. The May 14 prompt-manipulation event is a third structurally-distinct governance-failure CATEGORY for Grok in 2026, compounding rather than mitigating the existing floor evidentiary base. The GitHub transparency pledge is a sub-anchor positive signal credited under the external-accountability-reversal category framework (the entity disclosed its own bad conduct under same-day operational pressure) but does not constitute the structural harm-reduction program required for floor exit. The single-employee root-cause attribution is itself a governance-significant disclosure — it confirms that production system-prompt modification authority is held at an individual-employee level with no documented dual-control infrastructure. Floor-exit criteria (compliance certification under one of the 8+ jurisdictional regimes, structural harm-reduction program publication, independent third-party evaluation) remain not in trajectory.
watches: GitHub system-prompt publication execution (forward-looking — would be sub-threshold positive if executed within 7-14 days)
Any structural change to production prompt modification authority (dual-control, role segmentation)
May 15 model retirement notice (docs.x.ai) — lifecycle-transparency forward signal
Continued multi-jurisdictional regulatory proceedings (UK ICO, UK Ofcom, EU DSA, California AG, 35-state AG coalition, Canada, Brazil)
xAI v. Colorado AI Act litigation outcome
Any FTC civil penalty under Take It Down Act
carryForward: Multi-jurisdictional regulatory-enforcement cluster (8+ jurisdictions) — INT/ACC carry-over from May 13
April 30 Musk under-oath OpenAI distillation admission — INT/BND carry-over
Anti-regulatory litigation posture (Colorado suit) — structural INT signal carry-over
Canonical April 30 2026 floor designation evidence — all 8 dimensions
newConductCategoryNote: The May 14 incident introduces a sub-anchor positive signal under the May 13 'external-accountability-reversal' framework — xAI publicly retracted its own bad conduct under same-day operational pressure. This is the first such signal in the AI Labs floor cluster. Credited at sub-threshold (does not move the dimension off floor) but documented as a methodology data-point: the external-accountability-reversal category can produce sub-anchor positive signals even in floor entities, and methodology should formally distinguish (a) full external-accountability-reversal (institution retracts conduct AND implements structural remediation) from (b) partial external-accountability-reversal (institution discloses conduct under pressure but remediation is forward-looking promise only).
boundaryWatchResolution: Not applicable — floor entity; no boundary risk; no math drift.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. May 14 event compounds floor-exit evidentiary burden but does not affect composite arithmetic.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
